features.name: Task CRUD
features.description: Create, edit, and delete tasks with a required title and optional description and due date. Tasks are the core unit of work on the board.
features.userStory: As a solo planner, I want to create, edit, and delete tasks with a title, optional description, and optional due date, so that I can capture and maintain my personal to-dos.
acceptanceCriteria: User can create a task with a required title; submission is blocked if title is empty or whitespace-only.
User can add an optional description and an optional dueDate stored as an ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD).
User can edit any field of an existing task and changes are reflected immediately in the UI.
User can delete a task with a confirmation step, and it is removed from the board.
Each task has a stable unique id generated at creation time.

features.name: Three-column Kanban board
features.description: A fixed three-column board with columns 'To Do', 'In Progress', and 'Done' that renders all tasks grouped by their current status.
features.userStory: As a solo planner, I want to see my tasks organized into To Do, In Progress, and Done columns, so that I can visualize the state of my work at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Board renders exactly three columns in order: 'To Do', 'In Progress', 'Done'.
Each task appears in exactly one column based on its status field.
Columns display their name and the count of tasks within them.
Layout is mobile-first and scales cleanly up to desktop widths using Tailwind.
Empty columns show a subtle empty-state placeholder.

features.name: Drag-and-drop between columns
features.description: Move tasks between columns using @dnd-kit/core with full mouse, touch, and keyboard accessibility so status updates feel native.
features.userStory: As a solo planner, I want to drag tasks between columns using mouse, touch, or keyboard, so that I can update task status quickly and accessibly.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a task from one column to another updates its status to match the destination column.
Mouse, touch, and keyboard sensors from @dnd-kit/core are all enabled and functional.
Keyboard users can pick up, move, and drop a task using standard @dnd-kit keyboard interactions with visible focus.
The dragged task shows a clear visual overlay while in flight and the drop target column is highlighted.
Dropping outside any column cancels the move and leaves the task unchanged.

features.name: Persistence + overdue highlight
features.description: Persist the full board state to localStorage so it survives reloads, and visually highlight tasks whose dueDate is before today and whose status is not 'done'.
features.userStory: As a solo planner, I want my board to survive reloads and show overdue tasks clearly, so that I never lose my plan and can immediately spot what is late.
acceptanceCriteria: All task data and column assignments are written to localStorage on every mutation.
On app load, state is hydrated from localStorage; first-run users see an empty board without errors.
Tasks with a dueDate strictly before today's local date and status !== 'done' render with a distinct overdue visual style.
Overdue styling updates reactively when a task is moved to 'Done' or its dueDate is edited.
Corrupt or missing localStorage data is handled gracefully by falling back to an empty board.
